summary: Corrected gm_8017E48C from a misleading MinorScene* return to an int count, implemented the function in gm_17C0.c, and propagated that type through Adventure-mode callers and UnkAdventureData function pointers. The change removed a pointer-to-integer cast workaround in gmadventure.c and produced a new 100% match for gm_8017E48C worth +56 bytes according to the decomp.dev report.
what_changed: Implemented gm_8017E48C(MinorScene* scene) in src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.c as an int-returning count loop over gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes until the current scene idx is reached.
Added #include <melee/gm/gmadventure.h> to gm_17C0.c so the implementation can reference gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes and related MinorScene data.
Changed the gm_8017E48C declaration in gm_17C0.h from MinorScene* gm_8017E48C(MinorScene*) to int gm_8017E48C(MinorScene*).
Updated related helper declarations gm_8017E528, gm_8017E578, and gm_8017E5C8 to take int count instead of MinorScene* as their first parameter.
Named the relevant gm_8017CE34 parameter in gm_17C0.h as u8 count, clarifying the semantic role of the argument fed by gm_8017E48C.
Updated UnkAdventureData function pointer fields x4C, x64, and x68 in types.h to accept int as their first argument instead of MinorScene*.
Updated Adventure-mode callers in gmadventure.c to store gm_8017E48C results in int count variables and pass count into UnkAdventureData callbacks.
Removed the old local u8 wtf and the clang diagnostic suppression for -Wpointer-to-int-cast that had been masking an incorrect pointer-to-u8 cast.
smart_moves: Fixed the root type model instead of preserving casts: the PR converted gm_8017E48C and its downstream callback signatures to count-based int arguments rather than continuing to cast a supposed pointer.
Used callsite behavior as type evidence: callers pass the result into parameters and callbacks that behave like stage/adventure progression counts, not MinorScene pointers.
Kept the matching implementation compact: gm_8017E48C initializes count to 0, increments only when prior minor scenes have info.class_id == 2, and stops when the target scene idx is found.
Cleaned up decompiler scaffolding while matching, replacing a variable literally named wtf and a diagnostic suppression with the direct typed call gm_8017E48C(arg0).
Propagated the corrected type through both headers and struct function pointers, reducing the chance of future accidental pointer/int mismatches.
decomp_lessons: When a function's return value is immediately cast away from a pointer type, treat that as evidence the signature may be wrong rather than as a reason to add diagnostics or casts.
For unknown game-mode helpers, inspect all callback and struct-field uses before locking in a pointer type; a value passed into multiple numeric callback parameters may be a count or index.
A small matched function can unlock cleaner types in neighboring code: gm_8017E48C's implementation clarified gm_8017CE34 and UnkAdventureData callback signatures.
Semantic parameter names such as count can document decomp assumptions in headers without requiring full symbol recovery.
Synchronize function prototypes, struct function pointers, and all callsites in one PR when correcting a type; partial updates can leave misleading casts or callback signatures behind.
naming_conventions: Use count for the integer value returned by gm_8017E48C rather than retaining pointer-style temporaries such as temp_r27 or temp_r31.
Preserve address-based names for still-unknown functions such as gm_8017E48C, gm_8017E528, gm_8017E578, and gm_8017E5C8 while improving parameter types.
For struct callbacks still lacking semantic names, retain field-offset names like UnkAdventureData::x4C, x64, and x68 but correct their prototypes.
Avoid joke or frustration variable names such as wtf in committed decomp code once the underlying type issue is understood.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched gm_8017E48C as a loop that compares scene->idx against gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes[i].idx and counts entries whose info.class_id equals 2 before the match.
The corrected int return removed a pointer-to-u8 cast and aligned the C with expected register/value usage, enabling the function to match.
The decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/gm/gm_17C0 gm_8017E48C improved from 0.00% to 100.00%, +56 bytes.
Function prototype correction was part of the matching tactic: downstream callback signatures were changed to accept the same int count value.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback confirmed matched code stayed at 41.12% overall with +56 bytes and one new 100% function match for gm_8017E48C.
follow_up_queries: Check whether gm_8017E528, gm_8017E578, and gm_8017E5C8 implementations now have clearer parameter names or can be matched using the int count signature.
Audit other uses of UnkAdventureData callback fields x4C, x64, and x68 for opportunities to rename them based on their count and cpu_level behavior.
Search for remaining clang diagnostic suppressions or casts around gm_8017E48C-era Adventure-mode code that may indicate other incorrect signatures.
Investigate gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes info.class_id values to determine a more semantic name for the counted class_id == 2 scenes.
